7DS: Origin — KOR Performance Investigation & Q2 Strategy

Seven Deadly Sins: Origin (com.netmarble.nanaori / id6744205088) · KOR Android + iOS · Since launch Mar 23, 2026

📋 Prepared by: Haewon Yum (KOR GDS) 📅 Investigation date: 2026-04-14 🎯 Ticket: ODSB-17637

Executive Summary

Bottom Line (3 points)
  1. Login CPA campaigns beat the 5% D1 ROAS KPI on a since-launch average — Android 5.8% ✅, iOS 13.8% ✅ — but these figures include the high-efficiency launch burst and must not be read as the current run rate. CPI has risen +308% (Android) and +672% (iOS) since launch as KOR gaming audience exhausts. The AEO engine worked at launch; the live question is whether supply expansion (geo, iOS scale-up) can offset audience saturation.
  2. The ROAS campaign (iyH1) underperformance is entirely explained by cold-start — A2R normalizer frozen at 15.243 (zero variance, Apr 6–13); revenue model (revenue_a2r_consol_v1) not yet campaign-adapted — first retrain incorporating 7DS: Origin D7 labels expected ~Apr 14–21. D7 ROAS 1.05% is a model artifact, not a signal about the title's monetization quality.
  3. Structural monetization is thin but real — All-channel D7 ARPPU $16–$25 (iOS monetizes 35-49% better than Android); I2P 3–5% at D7. Revenue is there, but the game's light BM means D28+ is where real LTV accumulates.

1. Account & Title Context

Q1 2026 Backdrop — Netmarble Double Launch

Netmarble launched two new titles in March 2026. Both underperformed internal expectations within weeks, leading to all-media UA pullbacks. This created headwinds for Moloco's KOR GDS Q1 TA credit target.

DimensionStoneAge: Pet World7DS: Origin
Mobile launchMar 3, 2026Mar 23–24, 2026
Launch week market installs378K1.13M
Market install decline−70% in 4 weeks−97% in 2 weeks
Moloco launch spend$521K$127K
Netmarble internal read"Not bad — KOR only""A lot of concern about revenue"
D1 ROAS KPI9%5%
Moloco at launchYes (primary DSP)No — excluded pre-launch

7DS: Origin Budget Collapse Timeline

Mar 5, 2026
Original UA plan: ~$870K/month shared by Dabin Son
Mar 6 Biweekly
Final confirmed budget: $850K total (~$28K/day for 30 days). CTV budget: DV + Amazon, 20–24억 KRW, 10 days US-only. Moloco excluded.
Mar 23–24
Mobile launch — strong install volumes but near-zero D7 revenue. Google Play revenue rank #39 at Day 8.
Mar 26
All-media budget cut to ~$20K — across-the-board pullback. Console campaigns retained priority.
Apr 2 Biweekly
PA enabled ✅. D28 retention model proposed (test window: May–June). Q1 TA credit not achieved due to low spend volumes.
Apr 6
Moloco ROAS campaign launched: iyH1zVvUZpViudzo (KOR Android tROAS, $5.6K spend over 8 days)

Structural Issues at Launch

  • 9–16 GB download size — high friction before first login; CDN download event visible in postback data
  • Light BM — low-ticket purchases; monetization accumulates at D28+, not D1/D7
  • ADID attribution requirement — Netmarble counted only ADID-matched results internally, regardless of delivery
  • Cross-platform title — Console/PC absorbs marketing priority and budget

2. KOR Campaign Performance

2a — Overall Performance (KOR, since launch)

OSSpendInstallsCPIIPMCPMD1 ROASD7 ROAS
ANDROID$93,08319,182$4.850.148$0.725.8% ✅19.0%
iOS$42,62514,444$2.950.120$0.3513.8% ✅36.7%
Both OS exceed the 5% D1 ROAS KPI. iOS CPI ($2.95) and ROAS (13.8% D1, 36.7% D7) are exceptionally strong — iOS is the higher-quality inventory for this title.
Interpretation
  • Both OS beat the 5% D1 ROAS KPI even with the cold-start ROAS campaign (iyH1) included in the Android average. Strip iyH1 out and AEO-only Android ROAS is materially higher.
  • iOS unit economics are structurally stronger: $2.95 CPI (−40% vs Android), 13.8% D1 ROAS (2.4×). iOS KOR inventory is priced below its user quality — a durable advantage worth scaling before competing DSPs close the gap.
  • CPM is the CPI driver: Android $0.72 vs iOS $0.35 (2×), at comparable IPM (0.148 vs 0.120). Android competition in KOR gaming is heavier; iOS supply is underpriced relative to the revenue it delivers.
  • These are since-launch averages — launch-burst installs at lower CPI inflate them. Section 2b shows the CPI decay trajectory; current Android efficiency is materially below these headline numbers.
  • Strategic implication: as Android audience exhausts, iOS is the natural budget extension. Scale iOS before competing DSPs discover the pricing gap.

IPM Decay Root Cause CPI increased +308% (Android) and +672% (iOS) since launch. CPM only moved −18%/−1%. The driver is pure IPM collapse — audience exhaustion in the KOR gaming segment, consistent with launch-burst dynamics.

Moloco-attributed ARPPU tracks the market baseline on Android; iOS Moloco ARPPU is below market ($13.60 vs $17.49) — typical for scale-targeted audiences vs top-whale segment. D1→D7 I2P uplift is ~2× on both platforms, signaling strong late-payer conversion consistent with the game's light BM.

Interpretation
  • nl (Native List = Kakao feed) dominates Android spend share — expected given Kakao's reach in KOR, but VT% on Kakao is 93–96% (see action item: NHN exclusion). Watch CPI vs CT-only CPI on nl after excluding VT.
  • vi (Video Interstitial) drives the highest engagement formats on iOS — typically stronger I2P and ROAS for RPG titles in KOR where video creative quality matters more than format.
  • Exchange concentration risk: NHN accounts for material spend on Android with zero D7 revenue (see Section 4 cold-start analysis). Excluding NHN would improve Android ROAS optics significantly.
  • ⚠️ D1 ROAS by format/exchange will be available after re-running notebook Section 2e. Use for final creative budget allocation recommendation.

Why do the two Android CPA campaigns perform so differently?

Same creatives, same targeting, same KOR Android AEO goal — but the metrics diverge sharply. The root cause is a conversion event source change.

Primary — HTVA26Oz New — vHKyRhJl
KPI eventlogin_1stlogin_1st_s2s ⚠️
Launch dateMar 23, 2026Apr 7, 2026
Budget/day$400$1,143
CPI$4.35$14.44
D1 ROAS6.5%0.7%
Creative groupsIdentical

What changed: The new campaign switched from SDK-based login_1st to an S2S (server-to-server) postback variant login_1st_s2s. Since creatives and targeting are identical, the 3.3× CPI and 9× ROAS gap is not a supply or creative problem.

S2S Postback Investigation — pb table, Apr 7–15

Verified via moloco-dsp-data-view.postback.pb, app.bundle = com.netmarble.nanaori, Android, Apr 7–15 2026.

Date login_1st_s2s (attr.) login_1st SDK (attr.) login_1st_s2s total login_1st SDK total Attributed installs
Apr 71404,3394,737281,325223
Apr 81443,5435,310248,424143
Apr 91323,8586,136287,716127
Apr 101283,6185,835267,428149
Apr 111453,7426,434287,959161
Apr 121513,7326,816272,181165
Apr 13832,8955,260244,39865
Apr 14502,4953,769197,94337
Apr 15*111,2511,705116,7438

* Apr 15 partial day.

Key findings:

  1. S2S endpoint is live — 83–151 attributed login_1st_s2s events/day. Not a dead endpoint.
  2. 50× volume gap vs SDK — login_1st_s2s total (~5,000–6,800/day) is only ~2% of SDK login_1st (~260,000–284,000/day). S2S is not capturing the full login population — likely wired to a subset of MMP flows only.
  3. For Moloco-attributed users, S2S conversion rate is reasonable — cumulative 984 attributed S2S logins / 1,219 attributed installs (Apr 7–14) = ~81%. The signal arriving for Moloco users is not broken.
  4. AEO cold-start is the more immediate issue — at 8 days and ~1,200 attributed installs, the action model has insufficient history to identify high-value users. Poor early CPI/ROAS is expected; AEO typically needs 2–3 weeks to stabilize.
  5. Installs declining from Apr 13 — 65→37/day, potentially budget-capped or bid-floored out of auction as the campaign ramps down. Verify campaign is not throttled.
Two actions needed
  1. CSE check on S2S coverage: why is login_1st_s2s 50× lower than SDK login_1st? Confirm S2S is forwarding all qualifying events from the MMP, not just a subset.
  2. Do not scale the $1,143/day budget yet: allow 2–3 weeks of AEO learning before evaluating performance. Check if the campaign is being throttled/bid-floored after Apr 13.

4. ROAS Campaign Deep-Dive: Cold-Start Analysis (ODSB-17637)

Root Cause: Cold-Start Model (HIGH confidence — 6 independent evidence sources) At only 8 days old, the model lacks sufficient data to calibrate. Bid CPMs $0.05–$0.08 are 10–50× below exchange floor prices ($0.50–$2.00). 99.8% of bid candidates are rejected by FilterByBidfloor. This is a structural model maturity issue, not a signal about 7DS: Origin's monetization quality.

Campaign Performance Summary (Apr 6–13, 8 days)

Impressions
6.31M
Installs
113
CPI: $54.95
D7 Revenue
$65.02
17 AF revenue events
D7 ROAS
1.05%
Spend: $6,195

Evidence Chain

1
A2R Normalizer Frozen at 15.24 (zero variance)

p1 = p99 = 15.242939 across all 8 days. Revenue normalizers are baked into TFServing as a StaticHashTable and only update on model retraining — no daily update mechanism. Stonekey benchmark (same advertiser): A2R normalizer 29.93 (2× higher, regularly updated).

What does "A2R Normalizer Frozen" mean?

What is the A2R Normalizer?

A2R = Action-to-Revenue. When Moloco's model decides how much to bid for an impression, it needs to predict not just whether a user will convert (install, login), but also how much revenue that user will eventually generate. The A2R model does this: it takes the predicted action probability and multiplies it by an expected revenue per payer to estimate the user's LTV.

The normalizer is a scaling factor that converts the model's raw revenue prediction into a calibrated dollar value. Think of it as the model's internal reference point for "how much does a typical paying user of this app spend?" It is stored as a StaticHashTable inside TFServing (the ML model serving infrastructure) — meaning it is baked in at model deployment time and does not update in real time.

What does "Frozen" mean?

Frozen = zero variance across all days. The normalizer only refreshes when the full model is retrained and redeployed. For a brand-new title like 7DS: Origin, there is no historical revenue data yet, so the normalizer inherits either a default value or a stale value from the prior training run. The campaign ran 8 days and the value never moved — p1 = p99 = 15.242939 — proving it is a static baked-in constant, not a live estimate.

Why does this hurt bidding?

If the normalizer underestimates the title's true revenue potential, the model underestimates user LTV → bids too low → loses most auctions → the TCM multiplier inflates desperately to compensate → bids land on low-quality supply (NHN banners at $1.35–$2.00 CPM floors) rather than high-value placements. 7DS: Origin's normalizer (15.24) is 2× lower than stonekey's (29.93) — a title with 6 weeks of real revenue data — suggesting 7DS: Origin is being systematically undervalued.

Reference — spending_summary table

Source: moloco-data-prod.younghan.spending_summary

-- A2R normalizer: frozen vs calibrated comparison
SELECT
  date,
  campaign,
  ROUND(AVG(a2r_normalizer), 6)   AS a2r_normalizer,
  ROUND(MIN(a2r_normalizer), 6)   AS p1_normalizer,   -- should vary if live
  ROUND(MAX(a2r_normalizer), 6)   AS p99_normalizer,  -- p1=p99 = frozen
  ROUND(AVG(a2r_pred), 8)         AS a2r_pred,
  ROUND(AVG(wrapper_normalizer), 8) AS wrapper_normalizer
FROM `moloco-data-prod.younghan.spending_summary`
WHERE campaign IN (
    'iyH1zVvUZpViudzo',   -- nanaori ROAS (cold-start)
    'nazpxG3J5MareHRz'    -- stonekey ROAS (6wk benchmark)
  )
  AND date BETWEEN '2026-04-06' AND '2026-04-13'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1, 2

What the data showed

Campaigna2r_normalizerp1p99Status
iyH1 nanaori (8d)15.24293915.24293915.242939Frozen — zero variance
stonekey (6wk)29.93~28.1~31.4Live — varies daily

Fix timeline: a2r_normalizer updates when the weekly UA revenue model retrain (revenue_a2r_consol_v1) incorporates first campaign-specific D7 labels. D7 labels for the Apr 6 install cohort become available ~Apr 13–15; target retrain window ~Apr 14–21.

2
Revenue Model Predictions 21× Above Actual — Cold-Start Calibration Gap

The revenue model (revenue_a2r_consol_v1, context_revision=1775710817) was pre-trained on the broader portfolio before 7DS: Origin launched. Model predicts ~$12.6–12.9 revenue per install; actual D7 revenue per install = $65.02 ÷ 113 = $0.58 → 21× overestimate. No campaign-specific revenue signal in the model yet. First retrain incorporating real D7 labels expected ~Apr 14–21.

Daily model metrics trend (Apr 6–13) — spending_summary

Key Signals from spending_summary (daily aggregated, campaign iyH1zVvUZpViudzo)

Date a2r_normalizer a2r_pred ($/install) TCM wrapper_normalizer core_pred Impressions Spend ($)
Apr 0615.242912.611.340.013239.21e-5295,765181.8
Apr 0715.242912.011.650.012487.28e-5755,841535.7
Apr 0815.242912.011.990.013486.03e-5477,400477.4
Apr 0915.242912.853.380.018516.15e-51,057,966796.6
Apr 1015.242913.163.340.028696.13e-5819,838554.6
Apr 1115.242912.964.440.033906.38e-5835,483631.3
Apr 1215.242912.808.580.021935.99e-51,211,479772.7
Apr 1315.242912.896.540.016555.16e-5813,712374.5
Actual D7Revenue/install: $0.58 ($65.02 ÷ 113)Model 21× above actual → normalizer needs downward correction

Why the model is miscalibrated at launch

The a2r_normalizer (15.243) is inherited from the consolidated pre-training run on a broad portfolio of RPG apps. It represents the average revenue per payer from those apps, not from 7DS: Origin specifically. For a title with zero historical revenue data at launch, the model has no choice but to use this transfer value. The normalizer is baked into TFServing as a StaticHashTable — it can only update when the full model is retrained.

Weekly retrain cycle — when does it self-correct?

The UA revenue model (revenue_a2r_consol_v1) retrains weekly. The retrain uses D7 revenue labels — meaning it needs 7 days of post-install observation for each cohort. For the Apr 6 install cohort: D7 labels become available ~Apr 13–15. The weekly retrain incorporating these labels is expected ~Apr 14–21. After that retrain, the normalizer for com.netmarble.nanaori will be updated to reflect actual observed revenue, and bid CPMs should rise toward competitive levels.

Action model convergence signal (concern)

The action model (action_consol_v2_ifa_cpu_cont) is showing a sharp decline in pred/normalizer ratio: median 0.295 (Apr 6) → 0.137 (Apr 13) — nearly halved in 8 days. This means the model is predicting lower CVR per impression over time, likely learning that the initial user pool quality is declining post-honeymoon period. While some selectivity increase is expected, a halving in 8 days is steep and will contribute to underspend if TCM cannot compensate.

Important: Pred Calibrator does NOT apply here

The constant HISTORY_AOP_CALIBRATOR_ACTIVATION_WINDOW = 15 (in marvel2/.../ml_pred_calibrator_generation/query.py) refers to a tKPI-specific calibration path, gated in closure.go behind if tKpi > 0. Campaign iyH1zVvUZpViudzo uses goal type OPTIMIZE_ROAS_FOR_APP_UA — a standard ROAS goal, not a tKPI campaign. The pred calibrator does not apply. The "Apr 21" date referenced in earlier versions of this document was incorrect and has been removed.

Source query

SELECT
  date,
  ROUND(AVG(a2r_normalizer), 6)     AS a2r_normalizer,
  ROUND(MIN(a2r_normalizer), 6)     AS p1_normalizer,
  ROUND(MAX(a2r_normalizer), 6)     AS p99_normalizer,
  ROUND(AVG(a2r_pred), 4)           AS a2r_pred,
  ROUND(AVG(wrapper_normalizer), 6) AS wrapper_normalizer,
  ROUND(AVG(tcm), 4)                AS tcm,
  ROUND(AVG(core_pred), 8)          AS core_pred,
  SUM(total_imps)                   AS impressions,
  ROUND(SUM(total_spend_usd), 2)    AS spend_usd
FROM `moloco-data-prod.younghan.spending_summary`
WHERE campaign = 'iyH1zVvUZpViudzo'
  AND date BETWEEN '2026-04-06' AND '2026-04-13'
GROUP BY date ORDER BY date
3
TCM Inflation (1.34 → 8.58×): Budget Controller Cannot Close the Calibration Gap

The Target Cost Multiplier (TCM) is a budget pacing controller — it scales all bids up when the campaign is underspending. TCM inflating from 1.34 (Apr 6) to 8.58 (Apr 12) means the system is applying an 8.6× bid multiplier just to be competitive. Yet bid CPMs still only reach $0.05–$0.08 — far below exchange floors ($0.50–$2.00). This is direct evidence that the calibration gap (21× revenue overestimate + declining action model confidence) cannot be fixed by TCM alone. Only a model retrain with real campaign data will close it.

4
I2I Model Over-Predicting 2–4×

Calibration ratio 0.27–0.71 (model predicts 2–4× more installs than occur). Inflates bid values in theory, but dwarfed by the frozen/missing revenue components. Stonekey: 0.69–1.54 (well-calibrated, centered near 1.0).

5
Multiplier Trap: TCM Rose 1.3 → 8.6×

System desperately trying to spend budget. With I2I predictions ~0.00005 and uncalibrated revenue predictions, final bid CPMs only $0.05–$0.08 — far below exchange floors $0.50–$2.00. 99.8% of candidates rejected by FilterByBidfloor. TCM is a budget pacing multiplier, NOT a throttling indicator.

6
NHN Exchange Inefficiency: 31% Spend, $217 CPI, ZERO Revenue

NHN bidfloors $1.35–$2.00 CPM with 99.98% banner format. Cold model cannot steer spend away from inefficient exchanges. Recommendation: add budget cap or exclusion for NHN on this campaign.

Comparison: iyH1 (nanaori, 8 days) vs stonekey (same advertiser, 6 weeks)

MetriciyH1 nanaori (8d)stonekey (6wk)
D7 ROAS1.05%9.88%
CPI$54.95$48.38
I2I calibration ratio0.27–0.710.69–1.54 (healthy)
Predicted revenue/install$12.61–12.89 (model) vs $0.58 actual → 21× gap~Aligned (active revenue labels)
A2R normalizer15.24 (frozen)29.93 (2× higher)
Revenue wrapper multiplier1.68–2.42 (inflated)1.11–1.32 (calibrated)

Model & Calibration Charts

6a — A2R normalizer & prediction trend: iyH1 (cold) vs stonekey (calibrated)
6b — I2I install model calibration ratio over time (ratio < 1 = over-predicting installs)
7a — TCM escalation (1.3→8.6×) and daily budget instability
7c — Exchange efficiency: NHN highlighted. 31% of spend, $217 CPI, zero revenue.

Attribution / MMP Status

100% click-through, 0% view-through — no attribution anomalies. 100% attributed to Moloco. AppsFlyer revenue event working correctly (17 events, $65.02). No tracking issues detected.

Event Timeline

Apr 6, 2026
Campaign launched with ROAS goal type. Daily budget $980.
Apr 6–8
TCM rises 1.3→2.5 as system attempts to spend budget
Apr 6–13 (all days)
A2R normalizer frozen at 15.242939 (zero variance, p1=p99). Daily budget changed 5+ times: $980→$2,107→$230→$500→$980→$653.
Apr 9–11
TCM accelerates 3.4→5.8 — model increasingly desperate to spend
Apr 13
TCM reaches 8.6×. Bid CPMs still $0.05–$0.08 vs floors $0.50–$2.00. D7 ROAS = 1.05% — alert triggered (ODSB-17637).
~Apr 14–21 (projected)
Weekly retrain window: revenue_a2r_consol_v1 will incorporate first 7DS: Origin D7 revenue labels (available ~Apr 13–15). a2r_normalizer update expected. TCM should normalize once bids become competitive. Re-evaluate ROAS trajectory 7–10 days post-retrain.

5. GM Questions — Answered

From Kuyoung Chung's biweekly review request + Netmarble's competitor benchmark sharing (Apr 2026 biweekly)

Q1. Other channel performance comparison — how does Moloco compare to competitors?

Netmarble shared (approximate ranges, exact figures confidential):

ChannelCPI vs MolocoD1 ROAS vs MolocoStatus
KR_AOS_tROAS (competitors)50% cheaper4–5× higherRunning JP, US, KR + some WW
KR_AOS_AEO / Login opt40% cheaper2–3× higherLogin + tROAS optimization
KR_iOSMoloco onlyNo competitor running iOS
Context and Interpretation The competitor ROAS advantage on tROAS (4–5×) likely reflects mature campaigns vs Moloco's cold-start ROAS campaign launched Apr 6. The apples-to-apples comparison should be Moloco's CPA campaign (D1 ROAS 5.8–13.8%) vs competitors' tROAS results. Moloco iOS is the only DSP running — a strategic moat worth protecting. Q2 priority: get Moloco's Android ROAS campaign past the cold-start phase before drawing conclusions on ROAS competitiveness.
Q2. Should we switch from login CPA → Purchase CPA targeting? Revenue isn't materializing at expected pace.
Recommendation: Hold on login CPA — do not switch yet.

Rationale:

  • Login CPA campaign (HTVA) is performing above KPI: D1 ROAS 6.5%, D7 ROAS 20%+. The CPA machine is working.
  • Purchase CPA requires sufficient revenue signal for the model to optimize — with only 113 Moloco installs on the ROAS campaign and ~20K total on CPA, the purchase event volume is too thin for stable optimization.
  • All-channel D7 I2P is 3.2–4.6% (from fact_market_event) — payer conversion is happening, but at D7+. The game's light BM accumulates monetization over time.
  • Switching goal type now would reset model learning and delay any calibration further.

When to revisit: After the weekly model retrain (~Apr 14–21) — if ROAS campaign begins delivering meaningful revenue signal, evaluate Purchase CPA for the ROAS campaign. For CPA campaigns, D28 retention model is the more appropriate next goal type upgrade (proposed for May–June test).

Q3. ROAS campaign viability — is tROAS working? Should we keep it running?
Recommendation: Hold at minimum budget — do not kill, do not scale until model retrain completes (~Apr 14–21).

Current D7 ROAS 1.05% is a cold-start artifact, not a structural signal:

  • A2R normalizer is frozen at 15.243 (only updates at model retraining). Weekly retrain incorporating first D7 revenue labels expected ~Apr 14–21.
  • The identical mature campaign (stonekey, same advertiser, same goal) achieves 9.88% D7 ROAS.
  • Budget instability (5+ changes in 8 days) is actively harming model learning — recommend holding budget stable for at least 1 week after retrain.
  • NHN exchange should be capped or excluded immediately (31% spend, $217 CPI, ZERO revenue) — this can be done now without waiting for calibration.

Decision point: Evaluate ROAS performance again 7–10 days after the weekly retrain completes. If D7 ROAS remains below 3%, escalate to campaign goal review. If ROAS improves materially, scale gradually.

Q4. D28 retention model test — how does it work and when can we run it?

How it works: Moloco's D28 model optimizes bids for users predicted to generate revenue over a 28-day window, rather than D1/D7. For light BM games where monetization accumulates slowly (like 7DS: Origin), this model targets the longer-tail payers who are invisible to D7-based ROAS optimization.

Timing:

  • D28 matured data available: ~late May 2026 (from Mar 23 install cohort)
  • Model training + test setup: ~2–3 weeks
  • Estimated test window: June 2026
  • This aligns with Solo Leveling's 2nd anniversary (May/June) — a natural seasonality hook for broader Q2 testing

Prerequisites: April ROAS data reviewed (per Apr 2 biweekly agreement). Success/failure criteria redefined for 7DS: Origin's light BM context. Credit discussion with Hyejeong pending outcome of April review.

This is also the right vehicle to pair with a CTV incrementality test (PA enabled Apr 2 removes the historical blocker). Geo-split methodology available — pitch to Netmarble in May once April ROAS data is in hand.

6. Action Items

  • ★★★[IMMEDIATE] NHN exchange: add budget cap or exclusion on iyH1 ROAS campaign
    NHN consumed 31% of ROAS campaign spend ($1,951), delivered 9 installs at $217 CPI with ZERO D7 revenue. Cold model cannot steer away — manual intervention required now.
    Owner: Haewon / Campaign Ops | By: Apr 15
  • ★★★[IMMEDIATE] Stabilize ROAS campaign budget — freeze at consistent daily amount
    Budget changed 5+ times in 8 days ($980→$2,107→$230→$500→$980→$653). Each change resets model momentum. Hold stable at $500–$700/day (minimum meaningful learning) until the weekly model retrain completes (~Apr 14–21) and ROAS trajectory becomes readable.

  • ★★★[APR 14–21] Re-evaluate ROAS campaign post weekly retrain
    Weekly retrain window ~Apr 14–21. Set a decision-point review 7–10 days after retrain — if D7 ROAS improves meaningfully (target: >3%), scale budget gradually. If ROAS remains below 3%, evaluate switching to CPA goal or pausing. Monitor a2r_normalizer update as first signal.

    PA enabled removes the 18-month attribution objection. Solo Leveling 2nd anniversary (May/June) is the timing hook. Pitch geo-split incrementality test with test credits. CTV went to DV + Amazon for Origin launch — reclaim this with a structured test methodology.

    93–96% of Kakao installs are view-through. CT CPI is $43–$145 (vs apparent $1.70–$9.55). Prior GoT test showed near-zero incrementality. With PA live, a geo-split VT test is now feasible. Do not expand Kakao budget without incrementality validation.

    Do not switch from login CPA now — insufficient purchase event volume. Revisit once D28 install cohort matures and all-channel I2P signal is stronger. Current D7 I2P 3.2–4.6% is promising but thin for model optimization.

    Frequent budget changes (raise/reduce/raise pattern) harm Moloco model learning (flagged Jan 22 biweekly). Recommend: minimum 1–2 day hold after any budget change before next adjustment. Include in biweekly operating guidelines.
